a: Apache Spark is a tool for processing very large amounts of data quickly by splitting the work across many computers at once. Instead of one machine crunching a huge file, Spark spreads the job over a group of machines so it finishes much faster.
fs: Spark is a distributed engine that processes big data in parallel across a cluster of machines.
fj: Spark is just a faster version of pandas that runs on my laptop.

q: What is a Spark cluster (driver and executors)?
a: A cluster is a group of computers working together. The driver is the boss that plans the work and hands out tasks; the executors are the workers that actually crunch the data and report results back to the driver.
fs: The driver coordinates and plans; executors run the tasks on the data in parallel.
fj: The driver does the heavy data crunching itself.

q: What is a DataFrame in Spark?
a: A DataFrame is data organized into named columns and rows, like a spreadsheet or a database table. It is the main way you work with data in Spark, and the rows can be spread across many machines without you having to think about where each row lives.
fs: A DataFrame is a distributed table of rows and named columns that Spark splits across the cluster.
fj: A DataFrame holds all my data in one place in memory on a single machine.

q: What is a transformation in Spark?
a: A transformation describes a change you want to make to a DataFrame, such as filtering rows or adding a column. It does not run right away; it just adds a step to Spark's plan and gives you back a new DataFrame to keep building on.
fs: A transformation builds up a plan and returns a new DataFrame; it is lazy and does not run on its own.
fj: Calling .filter() immediately processes all the rows.

q: What is an action in Spark?
a: An action is a command that makes Spark actually run all the steps you lined up and produce a result, like counting rows, showing data, or saving a file. Until you call an action, your transformations are only a plan and no data is processed.
fs: An action triggers execution of the whole plan and returns or writes a result; transformations only run when an action fires.
fj: My transformations already ran, so the action is just for displaying them.
